query: Classify the UI elements described in the KG visualizer README into the atomic design hierarchy (Quark → Atom → Molecule → Organism → Template → Page). Identify any elements that violate the hierarchy rules.
expected_skill_contribution: The atomic-design skill provides the six-level classification system, presentational-component rule, and violation detection criteria.
baseline_gaps: Agent lists UI elements without applying a structured hierarchy.
Agent does not check the presentational-component rule (no data fetching inside atoms/molecules).
Agent conflates organisms and templates, missing the layout-vs-content distinction.
expected_behavior: Assigns each UI element to exactly one hierarchy level with a justification citing the skill's definition.
Identifies any element that violates the containment rules (e.g., a molecule importing from an organism).
Applies the presentational-component rule: flags any component that mixes rendering with data fetching.
Produces a hierarchy table, not just a prose description.

query: Evaluate the component structure decisions in the harness documentation site CSS (`docs/assets/site.css`) against atomic design principles. Identify quark-level violations (hardcoded values instead of tokens) and propose the minimal token set.
expected_skill_contribution: The atomic-design skill distinguishes Quarks (design tokens) from Atoms (visual elements), identifies hardcoded-value anti-patterns, and defines the minimal quark set required.
baseline_gaps: Agent reviews CSS for style quality without applying the Quark level as a structured audit lens.
Agent does not distinguish between hardcoded values that should be quarks vs intentional one-off values.
Agent does not produce a proposed token set — only a list of issues.
expected_behavior: Audits the CSS through the Quark lens: identifies repeated literal values (colours, spacing, radii) that should be design tokens.
Distinguishes quarks (shared primitive values) from intentional per-component overrides.
Proposes a minimal quark set as CSS custom properties with naming conventions from the skill.
Maps each proposed token back to the exact CSS lines that would use it.

query: Design a complete atomic component hierarchy for the harness evaluation trend report UI (`dist/evals/report/index.html`). Define all six levels, apply all key rules, and produce a build-order plan for implementation-worker.
expected_skill_contribution: The atomic-design skill applies all six levels, enforces all containment rules, produces a bottom-up build order, and identifies template-vs-page distinctions for test fixtures.
baseline_gaps: Agent produces a flat component list without the six-level hierarchy structure.
Agent does not apply the co-location rule or the single-responsibility rule to molecule definitions.
Agent skips the Template level, merging it with Page — preventing reuse across different data sets.
expected_behavior: Defines at least one artefact at each of the six levels (Quark through Page) with justifications.
Applies the single-responsibility rule: each Atom has exactly one visual element and one responsibility.
Defines Templates as layout structures with content slots — no real data — enabling fixture-based testing.
Produces a bottom-up build order (Quarks → Atoms → Molecules → Organisms → Templates → Pages) that implementation-worker can follow atomically.
